 {"id":"brenner_bot-uk5","title":"Implement global search with command palette","description":"# Task

Create a global search experience using a command palette (Cmd+K / Ctrl+K).

# Why Command Palette

- Familiar UX pattern (VS Code, Slack, Notion, etc.)
- Fast access from anywhere
- Can search across all content types
- Can also trigger actions (not just search)

# Search Scope

Search across:
1. **Transcript sections** - by text content
2. **Distillations** - by text content
3. **Quote bank** - by quote text
4. **Operators** - by name or definition
5. **Pages** - by title (Corpus, Distillations, Method, etc.)

# UI

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  🔍 Search or type a command...                    ⌘K       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  RECENT                                                      │
│  📄 §42: Choice of experimental object                      │
│  📄 §87: Abundance trick                                    │
│                                                              │
│  SUGGESTIONS                                                 │
│  📄 Corpus → complete_brenner_transcript.md                 │
│  📊 Distillations → Compare models                          │
│  ⚡ Method → Operator palette                               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

# Implementation

Use `cmdk` library (https://cmdk.paco.me/) - lightweight, accessible, customizable.

```tsx
import { Command } from 'cmdk';

<Command>
  <Command.Input placeholder='Search or type a command...' />
  <Command.List>
    <Command.Group heading='Recent'>
      {recentItems.map(item => (
        <Command.Item key={item.id}>{item.title}</Command.Item>
      ))}
    </Command.Group>
    // ...
  </Command.List>
</Command>
```

# Search Index

Build search index at build time:
1. Parse all markdown files
2. Extract searchable content
3. Store as JSON
4. Use client-side fuzzy search (fuse.js or similar)

# Keyboard Shortcuts

- `Cmd+K` / `Ctrl+K` - Open search
- `Escape` - Close
- `↑↓` - Navigate results
- `Enter` - Go to result
- `Cmd+Enter` - Open in new tab

# Actions (Beyond Search)

Command palette can also:
- 'Go to Corpus' → navigate
- 'Toggle dark mode' → action
- 'Start new session' → navigate + action

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Command palette opens with Cmd+K
- [ ] Search returns relevant results
- [ ] Results grouped by type
- [ ] Recent searches shown
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Actions work (navigation, etc.)
- [ ] Accessible","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:22:55.026628542-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:41:09.589330231-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T13:41:09.589330231-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented via SpotlightSearch.tsx - Cmd+K command palette with search across transcript, quotes, distillations, operators; keyboard navigation, actions, and accessibility","labels":["command-palette","search","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-uk5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:07.039014299-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-uk5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:28:02.327651525-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-ukd1","title":"EPIC: Semantic Search & Intelligence - Vector Search & Confound Detection","description":"# Semantic Search & Intelligence - Vector Search & Confound Detection

## Purpose
Add intelligent capabilities that make the system smarter over time: semantic search for relevant content, automatic confound detection, and learning from failure patterns.

## Components

### 1. Vector Embeddings for Corpus
Embed all Brenner content for semantic retrieval:
- Transcript paragraphs
- Distillation sections
- Quote bank entries
- Operator descriptions

This enables:
- "Find Brenner quotes about experimental design" (semantic, not keyword)
- Surface relevant wisdom based on hypothesis topic
- Match user hypotheses to similar discussions in corpus

**Technical**: Use local embeddings (no external API) or optional API.

### 2. Brenner Quote Semantic Matching
```typescript
async function findRelevantQuotes(
  hypothesis: HypothesisCard,
  operatorContext: OperatorType
): Promise<RankedQuote[]> {
  const contextEmbedding = await embed(
    `${hypothesis.statement} ${hypothesis.mechanism} ${operatorContext}`
  );
  
  const candidates = await vectorSearch(contextEmbedding, {
    index: 'brenner-quotes',
    limit: 20,
  });
  
  return candidates
    .map(q => ({
      ...q,
      operatorRelevance: computeOperatorRelevance(q, operatorContext),
    }))
    .sort((a, b) => b.operatorRelevance - a.operatorRelevance)
    .slice(0, 5);
}
```

### 3. Automatic Confound Detection
Different domains have predictable confounds:

```typescript
const CONFOUND_PATTERNS: Record<string, ConfoundType[]> = {
  'psychology': [
    { name: 'selection_bias', description: 'Who chooses to participate?' },
    { name: 'demand_characteristics', description: 'Do participants guess the hypothesis?' },
    { name: 'social_desirability', description: 'Are self-reports biased?' },
    { name: 'reverse_causation', description: 'Does Y cause X, not X cause Y?' },
  ],
  'epidemiology': [
    { name: 'healthy_user_bias', description: 'Do healthier people do X?' },
    { name: 'confounding_by_indication', description: 'Is X given to sicker patients?' },
    { name: 'temporal_ambiguity', description: 'Which came first?' },
  ],
  'economics': [
    { name: 'endogeneity', description: 'Is X determined by Y-related factors?' },
    { name: 'omitted_variable', description: 'What unmeasured Z affects both?' },
    { name: 'selection_on_outcome', description: 'Are we only seeing survivors?' },
  ],
};

function detectConfounds(hypothesis: HypothesisCard): IdentifiedConfound[] {
  const domain = classifyDomain(hypothesis);
  const patterns = CONFOUND_PATTERNS[domain] || [];
  
  return patterns
    .map(pattern => analyzeForConfound(hypothesis, pattern))
    .filter(result => result.likelihood > 0.3);
}
```

### 4. Failure Mode Learning
Track how hypotheses fail and surface patterns:

```typescript
interface FailureAnalytics {
  byDomain: Record<string, FailureModeDistribution>;
  byOperator: Record<OperatorType, CommonFailures>;
  byHypothesisStructure: PatternFailures[];
}
```

After enough sessions, surface insights like:
> "Hypotheses in psychology that don't address selection bias fail 73% of the time at the Exclusion Test phase"

### 5. Similar Hypothesis Matching
When user creates hypothesis, find similar past work:
- Other sessions with related topics
- Hypotheses that evolved similarly
- Evidence that might be relevant

This enables:
- Learning from other researchers' experiences
- Avoiding duplicate investigation
- Building on existing discriminative tests

## Privacy Considerations
All analytics should be:
- Aggregated (not individual sessions)
- Opt-in for cross-user learning
- Local-first where possible

## Dependencies
- Hypothesis Engine (provides hypotheses to match)
- Corpus infrastructure (provides content to search)

## Success Criteria
- Semantic search finds relevant Brenner quotes
- Confound detection catches common pitfalls
- Failure pattern learning provides useful warnings
- Similar hypothesis matching helps users learn

## Technical Options
- **Local embeddings**: Bun + transformers.js or wasm model
- **API embeddings**: OpenAI/Anthropic embeddings (optional, not required)
- **Vector DB**: SQLite with vector extension, or Postgres pgvector

-{"id":"brenner_bot-ukd1.1","title":"Create Vector Embeddings for Brenner Corpus","description":"# Create Vector Embeddings for Brenner Corpus

## Purpose
Generate vector embeddings for all Brenner content to enable semantic search and matching.

## Content to Embed

### Transcript Paragraphs
- Each meaningful paragraph from complete_brenner_transcript.md
- Preserve section numbers for citation
- Chunk appropriately (not too small, not too large)

### Distillation Sections
- All sections from the three distillation sources
- Tag by source (GPT, Gemini, Opus)

### Quote Bank Entries
- Individual quotes with attribution

### Operator Descriptions
- The four operators with their full descriptions

## Technical Options

### Option A: Build-Time Embeddings (Preferred for MVP)
- Generate embeddings at build time
- Store in static JSON file
- Use client-side similarity search
- No API calls required

### Option B: API Embeddings (Future)
- OpenAI embeddings API
- Anthropic embeddings
- Better quality, but requires API key

### Option C: Local Embeddings (Best for Privacy)
- Transformers.js with WASM model
- Runs entirely in browser
- May be slower

## Implementation

### Embedding Generation Script
```typescript
// scripts/generate-embeddings.ts
async function generateEmbeddings() {
  const transcriptChunks = await chunkTranscript();
  const embeddings = await embedAll(transcriptChunks);
  await saveEmbeddings(embeddings);
}
```

### Storage Format
```typescript
interface EmbeddingEntry {
  id: string;
  text: string;
  section?: number;  // For transcript
  source: 'transcript' | 'distillation' | 'quote' | 'operator';
  embedding: number[];  // Vector
}
```

### Similarity Search
```typescript
function findSimilar(
  query: string | number[],
  embeddings: EmbeddingEntry[],
  topK: number = 5
): EmbeddingEntry[];
```

## File Location
`apps/web/scripts/generate-embeddings.ts`
`apps/web/public/embeddings.json` (or similar)
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/search/embeddings.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All content embedded
- [ ] Embeddings stored efficiently
- [ ] Similarity search works
- [ ] Section citations preserved

 {"id":"brenner_bot-ukd1.2","title":"Implement Semantic Quote Matching for Sessions","description":"# Implement Semantic Quote Matching for Sessions

## Purpose
Surface relevant Brenner quotes based on the semantic content of the user's hypothesis and current context.

## Use Cases

### 1. During Hypothesis Intake
> User enters: "I think meditation reduces anxiety"
> 
> System finds: Brenner quotes about mechanism vs correlation,
> specificity of effects, measurement challenges

### 2. During Operator Application
> User is in Exclusion Test phase
> 
> System surfaces: §89 and related falsification quotes

### 3. During Evidence Recording
> User records a challenging result
> 
> System finds: Brenner quotes about being proven wrong,
> updating beliefs, the value of failure

## Implementation
```typescript
async function findRelevantQuotes(
  context: {
    hypothesis: HypothesisCard;
    phase: SessionPhase;
    operatorType?: OperatorType;
    recentAction?: string;
  },
  topK: number = 5
): Promise<RankedQuote[]> {
  // Build context string
  const queryText = buildContextQuery(context);
  
  // Get embedding
  const queryEmbedding = await embed(queryText);
  
  // Search quotes
  const candidates = await vectorSearch(queryEmbedding, {
    index: 'brenner-quotes',
    filter: buildFilter(context),
    limit: 20,
  });
  
  // Re-rank by phase relevance
  return candidates
    .map(q => ({
      ...q,
      relevanceScore: computeRelevance(q, context),
    }))
    .sort((a, b) => b.relevanceScore - a.relevanceScore)
    .slice(0, topK);
}

interface RankedQuote {
  section: number;
  text: string;
  relevanceScore: number;
  operatorRelevance?: OperatorType[];
}
```

## Quote Display Component
```typescript
interface BrennerQuoteSidebarProps {
  quotes: RankedQuote[];
  onQuoteClick?: (section: number) => void;
}
```

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/search/quote-matcher.ts`
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/BrennerQuoteSidebar.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Quotes match hypothesis semantically
- [ ] Phase-appropriate quotes surfaced
- [ ] Quotes link to full transcript
- [ ] Performance is acceptable

 {"id":"brenner_bot-ukd1.3","title":"Build Domain-Aware Confound Detection","description":"# Build Domain-Aware Confound Detection

## Purpose
Automatically detect likely confounds based on the hypothesis domain, helping users anticipate weaknesses.

## Domain-Specific Confound Libraries

### Psychology
```typescript
const PSYCHOLOGY_CONFOUNDS = [
  { name: 'selection_bias', description: 'Who chooses to participate?' },
  { name: 'demand_characteristics', description: 'Do participants guess the hypothesis?' },
  { name: 'social_desirability', description: 'Are self-reports biased toward socially acceptable answers?' },
  { name: 'reverse_causation', description: 'Does Y cause X, not X cause Y?' },
  { name: 'maturation', description: 'Would changes happen anyway with time?' },
  { name: 'regression_to_mean', description: 'Are extreme scores returning to average?' },
];
```

### Epidemiology
```typescript
const EPIDEMIOLOGY_CONFOUNDS = [
  { name: 'healthy_user_bias', description: 'Do healthier people do X?' },
  { name: 'confounding_by_indication', description: 'Is X given to sicker patients?' },
  { name: 'temporal_ambiguity', description: 'Which came first?' },
  { name: 'surveillance_bias', description: 'Are cases found more in studied groups?' },
  { name: 'immortal_time_bias', description: 'Must subjects survive to enter study?' },
];
```

### Economics
```typescript
const ECONOMICS_CONFOUNDS = [
  { name: 'endogeneity', description: 'Is X determined by Y-related factors?' },
  { name: 'omitted_variable', description: 'What unmeasured Z affects both?' },
  { name: 'selection_on_outcome', description: 'Are we only seeing survivors?' },
  { name: 'simultaneity', description: 'Do X and Y affect each other?' },
  { name: 'measurement_error', description: 'Are our measures biased?' },
];
```

### And more for: Biology, Sociology, Computer Science, etc.

## Implementation
```typescript
function detectConfounds(hypothesis: HypothesisCard): IdentifiedConfound[] {
  // 1. Classify domain
  const domain = classifyDomain(hypothesis);
  
  // 2. Get domain-specific confounds
  const library = getConfoundLibrary(domain);
  
  // 3. Analyze hypothesis for each confound
  const identified = library
    .map(confound => analyzeForConfound(hypothesis, confound))
    .filter(result => result.likelihood > 0.3);
  
  return identified;
}

function analyzeForConfound(
  hypothesis: HypothesisCard,
  confound: ConfoundType
): IdentifiedConfound {
  // Heuristic or ML-based analysis
  // Look for keywords, structural patterns
  return {
    ...confound,
    likelihood: computeLikelihood(hypothesis, confound),
    relevance: computeRelevance(hypothesis, confound),
  };
}
```

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/search/confound-detection.ts`
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/search/confound-libraries/`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Multiple domains covered
- [ ] Detection has reasonable accuracy
- [ ] Results integrated into hypothesis card
- [ ] Users can dismiss false positives

 {"id":"brenner_bot-ukd1.4","title":"FEATURE: Domain Templates - Pre-Configured Guidance by Field","description":"# Domain Templates - Pre-Configured Guidance by Field

## The Gap This Fills
Currently, the system treats all hypotheses the same. But different domains have:
- Different common confounds
- Different standard research designs
- Different effect size norms
- Different literature databases
- Different terminology

A psychology hypothesis needs different guidance than an epidemiology one.

## Feature Design

### Domain Template Structure
```typescript
interface DomainTemplate {
  id: string;
  name: string;  // e.g., "Psychology", "Epidemiology", "Economics"
  description: string;
  
  // Common confounds to check
  confoundLibrary: ConfoundType[];
  
  // Standard research designs
  researchDesigns: {
    name: string;
    description: string;
    discriminativePower: number;
    feasibility: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low';
    exampleUse: string;
  }[];
  
  // Effect size context
  effectSizeNorms: {
    small: number;
    medium: number;
    large: number;
    typical: string;  // Description of typical effects
  };
  
  // Literature resources
  literatureSources: {
    name: string;
    url: string;
    searchTips: string;
  }[];
  
  // Domain-specific Brenner quotes
  relevantQuotes: string[];  // Section numbers
  
  // Terminology
  glossary: { term: string; definition: string }[];
  
  // Level Split suggestions
  commonLevelSplits: {
    cause: string[];  // Common X decompositions
    effect: string[]; // Common Y decompositions
  };
}
```

### Pre-Built Templates

#### Psychology
```typescript
{
  name: "Psychology",
  confoundLibrary: [
    { name: "selection_bias", ... },
    { name: "demand_characteristics", ... },
    { name: "social_desirability", ... },
    { name: "reverse_causation", ... },
    { name: "maturation", ... },
    { name: "regression_to_mean", ... },
  ],
  effectSizeNorms: {
    small: 0.2,   // Cohen's d
    medium: 0.5,
    large: 0.8,
    typical: "Most psychology effects are d=0.2-0.4"
  },
  literatureSources: [
    { name: "PsycINFO", url: "...", searchTips: "..." },
    { name: "Google Scholar", url: "...", searchTips: "..." },
  ],
  commonLevelSplits: {
    cause: ["Self-report", "Behavioral", "Physiological", "Neural"],
    effect: ["Clinical diagnosis", "Symptom score", "Self-report", "Behavioral proxy"]
  }
}
```

#### Epidemiology
```typescript
{
  name: "Epidemiology",
  confoundLibrary: [
    { name: "healthy_user_bias", ... },
    { name: "confounding_by_indication", ... },
    { name: "temporal_ambiguity", ... },
    { name: "surveillance_bias", ... },
    { name: "immortal_time_bias", ... },
  ],
  effectSizeNorms: {
    small: 1.2,   // Odds Ratio
    medium: 1.5,
    large: 2.0,
    typical: "OR > 2 is considered strong in epidemiology"
  },
  researchDesigns: [
    { name: "Cohort study", ... },
    { name: "Case-control study", ... },
    { name: "Natural experiment", ... },
    { name: "Mendelian randomization", ... },
  ]
}
```

### Domain Selection Flow
At session start or hypothesis intake:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  What domain is your hypothesis in?                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│  ○ Psychology / Behavioral Science                         │
│  ○ Epidemiology / Public Health                            │
│  ○ Economics / Social Science                              │
│  ○ Biology / Medicine                                      │
│  ○ Computer Science / Technology                           │
│  ○ Physics / Natural Science                               │
│  ○ Other: [________________]                               │
│                                                             │
│  This helps us provide domain-specific guidance on:        │
│  • Common confounds in your field                          │
│  • Standard research designs                               │
│  • Effect size context                                     │
│  • Relevant literature sources                             │
│                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Integration Points
- Hypothesis Intake → Selects domain
- Level Split → Suggests domain-specific levels
- Exclusion Test → Generates domain-appropriate tests
- Scale Check → Uses domain effect size norms
- Confound Detection → Uses domain confound library

## Why This is Critical
Generic advice is less useful. Domain-specific:
- Catches field-specific pitfalls
- Uses appropriate standards
- Connects to relevant literature
- Speaks the domain's language

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 5+ domains pre-configured
- [ ] Domain selection at session start
- [ ] Templates affect all operators
